page: Microsoft Azure FX2ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The configuration includes 2 virtual CPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core. Cache resources consist of 48 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and a large 260 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 42.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 21.0 GB per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark results reveal a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The server demonstrates top-tier single-core capabilities, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2335.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 34.91 GB/sec. Conversely, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are generally weak. The Geekbench multi-core score is 2876.0, and the stress-ng multi-core CPU performance is rated as weak. Large-block memory read bandwidth is poor at 21.0 GB/sec, and write bandwidth is weak at 14.33 GB/sec.

The primary tradeoff of the FX2ms_v2 is its high memory density relative to its limited processing cores, which restricts its utility for parallelized compute tasks. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a high ratio of memory per vCPU, making it suitable for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing. Appropriate workloads include single-threaded applications, lightweight database operations, text processing, and memory-intensive tasks with low CPU concurrency requirements.
description: Microsoft Azure FX2ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs and 42.0 GB of RAM, running on a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it lacks local storage and GPUs but supports premium storage. Performance benchmarks highlight a strong contrast: single-core performance is top-tier, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2335.0 and strong single-threaded gzip compression, whereas multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are weak. The high memory-to-core ratio of 21.0 GB per core makes this instance qualitatively cost-effective for memory-heavy, low-concurrency tasks. It is best suited for single-threaded applications, text processing, and memory-bound workloads that do not require extensive parallel processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX2ms_v2 is a memory-intensive VM with 2 vCPUs, 42.0 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, offering strong single-core performance but weak multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX2ms_v2 is a memory-intensive VM with 2 vCPUs, 42.0 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for single-threaded tasks.
tagline: A memory-intensive virtual machine featuring high memory-to-core density and strong single-threaded performance for low-concurrency workloads.
